# Message Decision Packs

> Message Decision Packs, or MDP, is a local/offline standard, CLI, and plugin for making GTM messaging judgment explicit. It turns scattered product notes, ICP notes, source evidence, fit rules, claims, hooks, CTAs, avoid-rules, output rules, and eval fixtures into a versioned `.mdp/` folder that agents can inspect, route, validate, and use without rebuilding context from scratch.

> MDP is a decision/context layer. It is not an AI SDR, CRM, sequencer, enrichment provider, scraper, BI tool, or generic automation system. It does not send messages or update external systems.

Agents should start here when they need to understand the project quickly.

## Install

Latest release: <https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs/releases/latest>

Install the CLI and supported agent bundles:

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/install.sh) --agents -y
```

Install one supported host bundle and bootstrap the local `mdp` CLI when it is missing:

```bash
bash <(curl -fsSL https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/install.sh) --codex -y
bash <(curl -fsSL https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/install.sh) --claude-code -y
bash <(curl -fsSL https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/install.sh) --cursor -y
bash <(curl -fsSL https://mdp.orchidlabs.dev/install.sh) --opencode -y
```

## What MDP Gives Agents

- A local `.mdp/manifest.yaml` that declares pack identity, personas, channels, and routing shape.
- A `.mdp/sources.yaml` ledger that separates source facts, interpretations, and evidence gaps.
- Modular card files for personas, fit rules, pains, signals, positioning, approved claims, motions, channel policies, hooks, CTAs, avoid-rules, output rules, objections, gaps, and copy patterns.
- Optional prompt contracts under `.mdp/prompts/` for normalizing messy supplied rows and extracting reviewable card patches.
- CLI commands with stable JSON output so agents can validate, route, fit, brief, check claims, list gaps, and run evals.
- Plugin skills packaged through Pluxx for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode.

## Core Workflow

Use MDP as a decision system before drafting:

```text
source material
  -> source ledger
  -> pack cards
  -> mdp validate
  -> mdp fit
  -> mdp brief --context
  -> agent drafts only from routed context
  -> mdp check-claims
  -> mdp eval when behavior changes
```

When prospect context is missing, weak, disqualified, or unsupported, the agent should stop and surface the no-draft reason instead of smoothing gaps into confident copy.

## Important CLI Commands

```bash
mdp --json doctor --dir .
mdp --json validate --dir .
mdp --json --summary route --entries --dir . --persona "PMM" --job "linkedin outbound copy"
mdp --json fit --dir . --prospect <prospect.json>
mdp --json --summary brief --context --dir . --prospect <prospect.json> --channel linkedin
mdp --json check-claims --dir . --text "<draft copy>"
mdp --json gaps --dir .
mdp --json eval --dir .
```

## Plugin Skills

The plugin source lives in `plugin/` and is distributed through Pluxx. Important skills:

- `mdp-lfg`: end-to-end orchestration
- `mdp-create-pack`: create or improve a pack
- `mdp-icp-builder`: sharpen ICP, personas, and fit logic
- `mdp-source-extract`: turn supplied source material into card entries
- `mdp-message-angles`: codify hooks and angles
- `mdp-cta-builder`: codify CTA and reply-path policy
- `mdp-avoid-rules`: enforce category and claim boundaries
- `mdp-output-rules`: codify structure, style, and formatting constraints
- `mdp-prospect-brief`: turn provider-neutral prospect rows into fit decisions and briefs
- `mdp-copy-brief`: produce model-ready writing contracts
- `mdp-copy-eval`: evaluate generated copy against the pack
- `mdp-pack-review` and `mdp-pack-eval`: inspect pack quality and test behavior

## Canonical Public Examples

- [MDP for MDP](https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs/tree/main/examples/mdp-for-mdp): fastest public demo for explaining what a pack stores and how route, fit, brief, claim-check, gaps, and eval commands work together.
- [Profound GTM Vetting](https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs/tree/main/examples/profound-gtm-vetting): complete public-source pack showing ICP, target personas, fit rules, hooks, CTAs, guardrails, source-backed claims, gaps, and evals.

## Programmatic Access

- Repository: <https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs>
- Latest release assets: <https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs/releases/latest>
- Short context file: <https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs/raw/main/llms.txt>
- Full context file: <https://github.com/orchidautomation/message-decision-packs/raw/main/llms-full.txt>

Use `llms-full.txt` when you need deeper context about pack layout, command semantics, safety boundaries, examples, and agent behavior.
